---
title: "A25 Count vs State Population"
author: "Steven Todd McIntyre II"
image: "header.jpg"
date: "2026-03-05"
runtime: shiny
format: 
  dashboard:
    embed-resources: true
    theme: minty
    output:
      orientation: columns
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: false
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidytext)
library(here)
library(knitr)
library(shiny)
library(rio)
library(plotly)
library(usmap)

```

# Overview

## Column

**How useful can NTEE Codes be in Studying Arts Education Nonprofits?**

This dashboard will explore United States nonprofit organizations that are coded as "A25: Arts Education" in the National Taxonomy of Exempt Organizations (NTEE), a coding scheme that describes kinds of nonprofit organizations. "A25" is defined by the Urban Institute as "Organizations that provide informal arts educational programming and/or instruction but do not grant diplomas or degrees; or which offer services regarding the arts to educational institutions or to public entities involved in education." Using data from the [Urban Institute's Business Master File (BMF)](https://urbaninstitute.github.io/nccs/datasets/bmf/), I explore the kinds of organizations that define themselves as A25 and consider the NTEE classification's usefulness in exploring nonprofit arts education activities.

# Visualization 1: A25 Word Count

## Column

```{r}
a25 <- import(here("data/BMF_A25.csv"))

# set categories of top 50 words
kind_of_org <- c("foundation", "institute", "school", "center", "academy", "association", "guild", "society", "project", "studio", "incorporated", "club", "corporation", "alliance", "productions", "program", "council", "collective", "nfp", "league", "friends")
art_words <- c("arts", "art", "music", "creative", "fine", "design", "theatre", "performing", "workshop", "graphic", "dance", "traditional", "cultural", "hooking", "theater")
education_words <- c("education", "learning", "educational")
locations <- c("american", "community", "international", "county", "valley", "city")
target_audience <- c("artists", "youth", "kids", "artist", "childrens")

a25_top_50 <- a25 |>
  filter(ORG_YEAR_LAST >= 2024) |>
  unnest_tokens(output = word, input = ORG_NAME_CURRENT) |>
  select(word) |>
  filter(!word %in% stop_words$word) |>
  count(word) |>
  mutate(word = fct_lump_n(word, n=50, w=n)) |>
  filter(word != "Other") |>
  group_by(word) |>
  summarize(n = sum(n)) |>
  mutate(word=fct_reorder(word, n))

a25_top_50 <- a25_top_50 |>
  mutate(category = case_when(
    word%in%kind_of_org ~ "organization",
    word%in%art_words ~ "art",
    word%in%education_words ~ "education",
    word%in%locations ~ "location",
    word%in%target_audience ~ "people"
  ))

a25_kinds <- a25_top_50 |>
  filter(word%in%kind_of_org)

a25_top_50_plot <- a25_top_50 |>
  ggplot(aes(n, word, fill = category)) +
  geom_col(width = .9, position = position_dodge(.7)) +
  labs(
    title = "Top 50 Words in Arts Education Nonprofit Names",
    x = "Instances in Organization Names",
    y = "",
    caption = "Data from the Urban Institute's Business Master File."
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8, vjust=) 
  )

ggplotly(a25_top_50_plot)
```

# Visualization 2: US Map

## Column

```{r}
a25_state_count <- a25 |>
  count(F990_ORG_ADDR_STATE) |>
  mutate(state = F990_ORG_ADDR_STATE) |>
  select(state, n) |>
  filter(state != "")

a25_dist_map <- plot_usmap(data = a25_state_count, values = "n") +
  labs(title = "Where are the A25 Arts Education Nonprofits?") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_continuous(palette = "PuBu")

ggplotly(a25_dist_map)

```

# Visualization 3: A25 vs. State Population

## Column

```{r}
# join usmaps' state population table w/ a25 count data
a25_statepop <- inner_join(a25_state_count, statepop, by = join_by(state == abbr))

a25_statepop |>
  ggplot(aes(x=pop_2022, y=n)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Variation of Arts Education Nonprofits vs. State Population",
    
  )

```

# Summary of Updates

## Column

The US heatmap, while fun to look at, more or less looked like a population heatmap. Rather than making the heatmap per capita, which takes a relatively layman-friendly format and adds more complexity, it seemed interesting to put together a visualization that showed the number of arts education nonprofits against the state population with a fit line to see which states were above or below the line.

In this post, I did the following:

-   using the `usmaps` package, made a scatterplot of the population of each state vs. the number of arts education nonprofits in the state.
-   added labels to the scatterplot.
-   added an early filter setting to ensure I only captured currently active A25 orgs.
-   increased font size for org name words
